summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the value of 5! (5 factorial)?

n! (n factorial) = n × (n-1) × (n-2) × ... × 2 × 1. For example, 5! = 5×4×3×2×1 = 120. By definition, 0! = 1. The answer is 120. The other options — '25', '60', and '15' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the value of P(6, 2)?

Permutations P(n,r) = n! / (n-r)! count arrangements where order matters. Substitute the given values and simplify. The answer is 30. The other options — '15', '36', and '12' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the value of 0! ?

n! (n factorial) = n × (n-1) × (n-2) × ... × 2 × 1. For example, 5! = 5×4×3×2×1 = 120. By definition, 0! = 1. The answer is 1. The other options — '0', 'Undefined', and '−1' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the value of C(8, 0)?

Combinations C(n,r) = n! / [r!(n-r)!] count selections where order doesn't matter. Substitute the given values and simplify. The answer is 1. The other options — '8', '0', and '56' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
